rag_rules: Latched expressions must appear only in component implementations, not in type scopes or invalid contexts.
Binary expressions in guarantees must have compatible boolean types on both sides; avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE clauses are visible or properly declared as helpers to prevent '<error>' type issues.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible component features or declared helpers to avoid type resolution errors.
Validator diagnostics pointing to '<error>' types indicate unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations that must be fixed at the source.
Component implementations are the correct scope for latched expressions and implementation-specific guarantees.
Guarantee expressions must maintain type consistency; binary operators like 'and' require boolean operands.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent type errors.
Place latched expressions and implementation-specific logic in component implementations, not in type annexes.
Ensure all identifiers in guarantee expressions are properly typed and resolved before validation.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing latched expressions in component types or using invalid subcomponent ports in type-level contracts.
Do not attempt to resolve '<error>' types by adding architecture; instead, trace and fix the root cause of unresolved references.
